tenders.title: Stormwater Management Project (SWMP)  Feasibility and Design Study – Mill Brook
match_reasons: matched keywords: design study, hydraulic, stormwater
tenders.classification_note: Looks like a consulting, design, study, assessment, or engineering-services opportunity.

raw.title: Stormwater Management Project (SWMP)  Feasibility and Design Study – Mill Brook

unspscLevelData.unspscLevel1: Management and Business Professionals and Administrative Services
unspscLevelData.unspscLevel4: Feasibility studies or screening of project ideas

raw.description:
The work covered under this RFP is for the completion of the “Stormwater Management Project Feasibility and Design Study - Mill Brook” for the Town of Kentville. The project will include community engagement, technical assessments, hydrologic and hydraulic analysis, environmental review, conceptual infrastructure design, and implementation planning associated with stormwater management and flood mitigation within the Mill Brook watershed area.

This project is intended to provide recommendations based on existing conditions, projected climate impacts, future land use considerations, and evolving infrastructure needs. While previous studies provide valuable foundational information, this project will establish a site-specific framework to address current deficiencies and future climate resilience requirements.

attachments.filename: TOK RFP - Mill Brook Stormwater Feasibility and Design Project Final-0375672.pdf

tenders.title: Feasibility Study for Nova West Regional Business Park Phase 3 Expansion
tenders.procurement_entity: Municipality of the District of Yarmouth
tenders.classification_note: Looks like a consulting, design, study, assessment, or engineering-services opportunity.

raw.title: Feasibility Study for Nova West Regional Business Park Phase 3 Expansion

unspscLevelData.unspscLevel1: Engineering and Research and Technology Based Services

procumentEntityData.name: Municipality of the District of Yarmouth
procumentEntityData.nameFr: Municipality of the District of Yarmouth FR

raw.procurementEntity: Municipality of the District of Yarmouth
raw.description:
The Municipality of the District of Yarmouth (MODY) is seeking proposals from qualified multidisciplinary consulting firms to undertake a feasibility study for the potential Phase 3 expansion of the Nova West Regional Business Park (NWRBP). The study will evaluate approximately 28.1 hectares (69.4 acres) of municipally identified lands to determine development feasibility, environmental and regulatory constraints, servicing requirements, infrastructure considerations, implementation options, and phased development opportunities to support future industrial and commercial growth within the Municipality.

The feasibility study will include a phased assessment approach consisting of high-level screening, environmental and planning review, geotechnical and climate resilience considerations, conceptual servicing and infrastructure analysis, utility coordination, development phasing, and cost estimates.

procumentEntityData.tenderPublicOpenLocation: Council Chambers Or Conference Rm Depending On Availability

tradeAgreement.name: TA-12
tradeAgreement.description: Comprehensive and Progressive Agreement for Trans-Pacific Partnership (CPTPP)

raw.description:
Sealed quotes addressed to the Town of Antigonish at 274 Main Street, Antigonish, Nova Scotia, will be received up to 2:00 pm June 10, 2026, for tender Active Transportation Corridor Improvements -  Highland Drive to Church Street, Tender TOA2026-02. Tender packages may be obtained at the office of the Department of Infrastructure and 
Engineering at Town Hall, 274 Main St, Antigonish, Nova Scotia.
The Work can be generally described to include: the removal of existing concrete curb, gutter and sidewalk, installation of new concrete curb & gutter and trail, removal of existing street asphalt as required, and street asphalt 
replacement. The work area runs along St Ninian Street from Highland Drive to Church Street. Work requires all traffic control, adjustments to catch basin and manhole covers, valve boxes and related work.
The Town reserves the right to waive any informalities or to reject any or all quotes based on changes to the approach to the work, organizational and perceived liability considerations, the financial suitability of proceeding with the execution of the work, individual tenderer's level of experience, available personnel and equipment, the Owners perception of the impact of performance on similar projects, or potential performance problems in keeping schedule targets. The Town reserves the right to omit portions of the work should it be deemed necessary for overall financial reasons.

attachments.filename: TOA2026-02 AT Highland to Church Specification (1)-1837379.pdf

raw.memo:
Submit completed tender form for above project in sealed envelope marked as follows:
TENDER
Active Transportation Corridor Improvements ñ Highland Drive to Church Street
TOA2026-02
Town of Antigonish
Lindsay Basinger, Project Manager
274 Main Street
Antigonish, N.S.
B2G 2C4
and must be delivered to the following address up until 2:00 p.m., local time, on June 10th, 2026,
hereinafter referred to as the Tender Closing.
274 Main Street
Antigonish, N.S.
B2G 2C4
Attention: Lindsay Basinger

Engineering and Research and Technology Based Services

raw.description:
Halifax Harbour Bridges (HHB), operating as a division of Link Nova Scotia, is the
issuing public body and procuring authority for the purposes of this Request for
Proposal. HHB is seeking proposals from qualified proponents to provide engineering
services for the assessment and detailed design of navigation and aviation lighting
system upgrades on the A. Murray MacKay Bridge (AMM). The proponent shall be
responsible for reviewing existing conditions, defining technical requirements and
performance specifications, and preparing design and tender documents for the
proposed upgrades.
